text.title: Essential Portugal: 2 Week Travel Guide & Itinerary (Porto, Lisbon, Algarve, Sintra + Douro Valley)
text.u: if you're planning a trip to Portugal or
thinking about it I just got back from a
2-e trip and I'm going to go over my
full itinerary because I think it was
the perfect trip for firsttime visitors
we saw a lot of the highlights with a
few of the Hidden gems sprinkled in we
covered some of the country's most
famous spots from the historic beauty of
Porto to the sundrenched beaches of the
Algarve and lots in between so I'm going
to share where we stayed and for how
long what we did what was worth it and
wasn't worth it and also what I would do
differently the next time I go back I'll
start with an overview of our schedule
we flew into Porto and stayed four
nights then went to Lisbon and stayed
four nights there and then we went to
the Algarve and stayed four nights there
and then flew home from Pharaoh many
people buy roundtrip flights to Lisbon
which is usually the most affordable and
convenient option however I'm so glad we
planned our trip this way because we
didn't have to do any backtracking or
have any was Ed travel days I highly
recommend doing it this way if you can
we were able to do this because I
actually found premium economy flights
that were cheaper than economy seats so
my daily drop Pro membership is
definitely worth it I'll leave a link
below if you want to check it out for
yourself all right so we started in
Porto and basically the way our schedule
worked out was that we had about 2 and
1/2 days to explore the city I loved
Porto so much it has this amazing mix of
vibrant history old world architecture
almost medieval esque and the food and
wine scene is chef's kiss speaking of
that we took a food tour on our first
morning which was definitely the way to
go
reses we loved strolling the iconic
neighborhoods like the rera district
with its colorful narrow streets there's
so many great viewpoints to explore lots
of great wine bars the Maro doala was
fun to visit this is the original Open
Air Market where you can browse all
kinds of vendors you can get a glass of
wine while you shop and peruse it's just
a fun and Lively atmosphere even if you
don't plan to buy anything and of course
you have to visit the Old Port Wine
sellers do some tours some tastings it's
really fun then on our last day we took
a day trip to the Duro Valley the
birthplace of port wine for me this was
a highlight of the whole trip it's such
a Scenic part of the country absolutely
breathtaking we found this great small
group tour and our guide was actually a
Somalia he brought us to two wineries
that were totally different from each
other in the best way oh oh it's really
[Music]
cool we went on a boat ride along the
Duro River and and then checked out some
awesome viewpoints of the Valley's
terraced Vineyards and gorgeous
Landscapes honestly this is a must do if
you come to Porto non-negotiable I would
even say next time I come back to
Portugal though I'm definitely saving at
least two or three nights for the Duro
Valley it was
[Music]
beautiful we booked this trip on get
your guide so I will leave a link in the
description below or you can also scan
the QR code in Porto we stayed at an
apartment hotel called Victoria's
Terrace Apartments it had really good
reviews and I know why now it's in the
perfect location right on the edge of
the Oldtown area so perfectly walkable
to everywhere but nice and quiet at
night and the view from our balcony was
actually better than one of the Hidden
Gem viewpoints that was just down the
street from us so that was pretty cool
and the couple that runs this place are
so kind and friendly they give us all
kinds of recommend
for places nearby like we went to a
photo show that was 2 minutes away and
it was an unforgettable
[Music]
evening there's this awesome little
Hidden Gem Terrace bar called rooftop
Flores go there for Sunset bevies and
there's also great restaurants within 5
or 10 minutes I really liked C Tina 32
oh and I cannot forget franchina this is
a Porto specific food it's just
something you have to try it's bread and
two or three kinds of meat and there's
cheese and an egg on top and a tomato
beer sauce we found awesome ones at this
restaurant that I cannot pronounce but
their whole menu looked great as well
overall I think 2 and 1/2 days in Porto
was enough I would absolutely stay 3 or
4 days if I had more time but I think
you do need at least one and a half to
two full days to really experience the
city plus a full day dedicated to the
duroro
valley on day five instead of taking the
train straight to Lisbon we actually
hired a driver and then that way we were
able to turn the transfer day into a
full sightseeing day and this was
honestly one of the best decisions of
our trip so I'll leave the link to vur
where we found our guide and driver he
picked us up from our hotel in Porto at
9:00 a.m. and then we arrived in Lisbon
by 6:00 p.m. where we made three stops
along the way first we stopped in AO
which is known as the Venice of Portugal
because of its canals and colorful boats
it's a pretty little town as well with
lots of beautiful buildings in art Nuvo
style it's also known for a traditional
Suite called
ooes not sure how to pronounce it but
something we learned was you always have
to try the local pastry when you're in
Portugal our next stop was nazer a
coastal town famous for its massive
waves that attract Surfers from all over
the world usually from November to
February is where you can see the
highest waves on Earth sometimes
reaching as high as 30 m while we
weren't visiting in big wave season the
viewpoints overlooking the ocean and the
beach are absolutely breathtaking and
definitely worth the stop the village
itself is also quite picturesque and the
pink marble cathedral was definitely one
of the
highlights finally we stopped in obidos
a small medieval Town enclosed by Castle
stone walls that you can actually walk
on top of I loved this little town so
much it's cobblestone streets flower
lined alleys historic charm little shops
it was just so adorable and it's also
known for its Cherry lure Ginger which
is served in little chocolate cups and
there's little ginger stands every 20 or
feet they're only about one or one and a
half years per shot so I definitely
recommend trying out a few I would have
loved to spend at least half a day here
in obidos next time maybe even stay in
the castle which is now actually a
fourstar hotel so if you only have time
for two stops I would probably skip AO
and if I only had time to visit one
place I would definitely choose
obidos we then arrived in Lisbon where
we checked into our hotel and stayed for
the next 4 nights we stayed at this
apart hotel called Ando living once
again I booked it because of the amazing
reviews and they were totally right this
hotel was in the perfect location in the
baija neighborhood so we were within
walking distance or easy public
transport to all the main attractions in
the old neighborhoods it was also nice
and quiet at night it was so clean new
and modern reasonably priced and the
little welcome package of wine and
goodies was a lovely touch and literally
around the corner was the Green Street
area which is three blocks of little
authentic bars and restant resturants
that open up in the evening it's kind of
similar to Pink street but the better
more authentic Hidden Gem among locals
so then we spent the next two full days
exploring Lisbon on our first day we
took a halfday tuck tuck tour this was a
fantastic way to get introduced to the
city our guide took us through Lisbon's
famous neighborhoods Alama bisha bario
elto and chiato Lisbon is also filled
with viewpoints or Mirad duros and our
guide took us to some of the best ones
for the best views of the city he also
took great photos of us which turned out
to be some of my favorites from the
whole
trip we finished our tutic tour in bam
home to the BM Tower built to defend
Lisbon against possible attacks and
invasions and it's one of the most
unique monuments in Lisbon and there's
nothing quite like it in other European
cities as well there's the gonos
monastery which is a gorgeous Portuguese
Gothic building that was built in the
17th century this is also home to the
birthplace of the famous pases de balm
the original recipe of pastel denata
everyone said these were the best of the
best the Portuguese custard Tarts and
yes I can confirm they are in A League
of Their Own we booked this trip through
get your guide so I'll leave the link
below to the one that we booked or you
can scan the QR code I also just want to
say that none of this trip was sponsored
we paid for everything ourselves from
the hotels to the guides to the drivers
to the restaurants everything Lisbon is
a great City to just wander get lost
take in the sights snap some fun photos
and stop for food and bees along the way
and that's exactly what we did we really
liked the carel rooftop bar for Sunset
as well and if you like espresso
martinis you have to visit cassa do
pastel deata leor for a special lisban
Twist on this yummy cocktail
[Music]
we also took in another foto show the
old neighborhoods here in Lisbon is
where this originated foto is a
traditional style of Portuguese music
characterized by mournful tunes and
lyrics often expressing a sense of
longing Nostalgia or sadness I would say
catching a photo show is a must do we
chose to do dinner and the photo show at
Bohemia LX but you can also find smaller
venues and stop in and have maybe a
glass of wine while you listen to the
Beautiful music
[Music]
all right now here are a few things that
I thought were a little overrated in
lisbin first was the timeout Market to
me this was just a food court but
perhaps if you're in town for only a few
hours maybe from a cruise and you want
to try a bunch of different Portuguese
Foods this would probably be a good stop
I also wasn't crazy about pink Street
maybe I'm too old it was loud busy and
full of
influencers and waiting in line for the
Santa useda lift it was really cool to
look at it's very interesting
architecture for sure but you can
actually find stairs to go up for free
or you can also get a great view of it
from the carel rooftop I probably could
have used an extra day in Lisbon but
because we saw Porto which I kind of
preferred the more laid-back vibe there
2 days was enough for me in Lisbon I
will say Lisbon was very busy when we
were there in mid October so I can't
even imagine how busy it gets in the
summer
months our last and Lisbon was dedicated
to cintra a fairy tale Town nestled in
the Hills about 45 minutes from the city
this is one of the most popular day
trips from Lisbon and a place that you
absolutely have to see with your own
eyes there are several stunning palaces
to visit here and you won't have time to
see them all in one day so you will have
to choose we visited two of the most
famous palaces starting with the
colorful Penna Palace which looks like
something straight out of a story book
it's got colorful Towers an Eclectic
[Music]
architecture we then toured the
intricate Kint de regula a romantic
estate with beautiful gardens
underground tunnels and the famous
initiation well we booked a private tour
and this was absolutely worth every
penny because cintra is huge there is so
much more than just a few palaces to
visit our guide not only brought us
everywhere but came with us and
explained all the history and all the
stories behind everything we were
looking at I am so glad we hired a guide
this whole area is breathtaking but also
quite overwhelming especially with the
crowds there were so many people without
a guide that were kind of just aimlessly
wandering around and a lot of them were
constantly trying to listen into what
our guide was sharing with us so I'll
leave a link to the guide that we booked
if you booked with him let him know Al
Smalls sent you and if he's not
available I also left links for a couple
other highly rated guides and
tours for our final leg of the trip we
took a 45-minute flight from Lisbon to
Pharaoh a city in the algar we got
ourselves a rental car and then drove to
caruo our home base for the last four
nights we chose caruo because it's
centrally located which made it really
easy to explore the other towns beaches
and hiking trails along the algar coast
the town itself is super Charming with
its whitewashed buildings and a
beautiful Coastline we stayed at a six
suet Guest House Bed and Breakfast
called Kint do ores when I tell you it's
one of the best accommodations I have
ever stayed in I am not exaggerating
it's run by the sweetest family who are
so kind and full of great
recommendations for what to do in the
area each morning guests are treated to
a massive breakfast spread and any type
of coffee that you want there's a large
heated pool with all kinds of room for
sunbathing plenty of common areas to
hang out and relax in and all the rooms
have private patios with kitchens I will
100% be booking a vacation just to stay
here again we only had four nights and I
wish we had 10 more one thing we quickly
realized after getting to the Algarve
was that four nights was nowhere near
enough but here were the highlights for
us in that time one of our favorite
spots was algar SEO this is a spot with
Incredible rock formations and natural
Gros all along the coast you can take
some amazing photos and even Explore all
the little hidden caves it's only 5
minutes from the hotel as well we also
hiked 8 km of the seven hanging valleys
Trail which offers some of the best
coastal views in all of Portugal the
trail runs along the cliffs above the
ocean passing by Scenic viewpoints
hidden Coes and secluded beaches this is
a must do and was a highlight of the
whole trip for
me another highlight was kaying to the
benel C caves we were able to get so
much closer than the bigger boat tours
we took a guided tour and we saw so many
other caves while we kayaked along the
coastline highly recommend doing this
but it's definitely a bit of a workout
so be prepared for
that we spent some time visiting some of
the most famous and beautiful beaches
and postcard views near Lagos in albuera
each beach in The Algarve has its own
Vibe and so exploring a few of them
really adds to the experience
[Music]
there's also a lot of cute towns and
Villages all over I really loved farago
an adorably picturesque town with
whitewashed houses built in the Hillside
and flowers all through the streets also
a great spot for souvenir
shopping overall it was an amazing trip
and I think 12 days was a good amount of
time to cover a lot of ground without
feeling too rushed especially because we
made the most of our travel days I went
on this trip with my mom and sister and
we all took one carry-on bag and one
personal item each and that was
definitely the way to go these are the
bags that we used here that fit on all
the airlines that we flew I'll leave
links to the bags but I also made a
video talking about them more in depth
and we also used sling bags I used the
large Sling from Nomad Lane my sister
used the Metro Sling from monos and my
mom used her belt bag from Lululemon
each of these were perfect size and
really nice and secure so I'll leave
links to the those as well I also put
this itinerary up on my travel blog with
a little more detail if you want to find
out more and keep checking back because
I'm going to be adding more posts about
Portugal I'm also working on a Portugal
pocket guide that will be full of all my
tips and research and recommendations so
I'll announce that here on my channel
when it's ready I hope you found this
video helpful let me know in the
comments when your upcoming trip to
Portugal is thank you so much for
watching and I'll see you in the next
one
[Music]
